Custom Wrapper for ggsave with Sensible Defaults

ggsave_custom.Rd

Save a ggplot or patchwork object with cleaner defaults (BRAP Journal style).

Usage

ggsave_custom(
  save_name,
  inplot = NULL,
  imgpath = ".",
  wdth = 7,
  hght = 4.1,
  unts = "in",
  bgcol = "white",
  dpi = 600,
  web_suffix = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

save_name

File name (e.g. 'plot.png', 'plot.pdf') to save.

inplot

Plot object. If NULL, saves the last displayed plot.

imgpath

Directory path where the plot is saved (default: current directory).

wdth

Width of the plot in units (default: 7).

hght

Height of the plot in units (default: 4.1).

unts

Units for width/height (default: "in").

bgcol

Background color (default: "white").

dpi

Resolution in dots per inch (default: 600).

web_suffix

If TRUE, also saves a low-res version with "_web" suffix (default: FALSE).

...

Additional arguments passed to ggsave().

Value

Invisibly returns full file path to saved image.

Examples

# Example usage:
dotforest <- create_forest_dot_plot(
  prepare_forest_dot_data(effects_table)
)
ggsave_custom("dotforest.png", imgpath = tempdir(), inplot = dotforest)
#> Plot saved to: /tmp/RtmpLT1kQg/dotforest.png

On this page

Developed by Lovemore Gakava, Chen Chen, Abigail Mabe, Michael Colopy, Brian Waterhouse.
